description:
Perform a hydraulic analysis to estimate the flood wave celerity (speed) and mean water velocity for the White River at Muncie using the Kleitz-Seddon principle (c = dQ/dA).

1. Open the Muncie project results (`Muncie.p04.hdf`) using Python.
2. Locate the Cross Section at River Station '15781.9'.
3. Extract the Flow (Q) and Flow Area (A) time series for this cross section.
4. Isolate the data corresponding to the **Rising Limb** of the flood event (from the start of the flow increase up to the peak flow).
5. Compute two values based on this rising limb data:
   - **Wave Celerity (c)**: The slope of the linear regression of Flow (y-axis) vs. Flow Area (x-axis).
   - **Mean Water Velocity (V)**: The average of the instantaneous velocities (Q/A).
6. Save the extracted rising limb data to `~/Documents/hec_ras_results/celerity_data.csv` with columns: `Time_Index`, `Area_sqft`, `Flow_cfs`, `Velocity_fps`.
7. Save the calculated results to `~/Documents/hec_ras_results/celerity_analysis.txt` in the following format:
